name: D-Mannose
summary: Decision-ready summary: D-Mannose is best framed for recurrent UTI prevention context; urinary tract adhesion support; safety screen: diabetes medication review; active UTI needs medical care.
description: D-Mannose supports urinary tract; UTI prevention context with moderate confidence in this merged decision layer.
mechanisms: Prevents bacterial adhesion (E. coli) to urinary tract epithelium.
raw_mechanisms: Prevents bacterial adhesion (E. coli) to urinary tract epithelium.
unmapped_mechanisms: Prevents bacterial adhesion (E. coli) to urinary tract epithelium.
dosage: 2 g/day was tested for 6 months in a large UK primary-care RCT and did not significantly reduce medically attended recurrent UTI
typical_dosage: 2 g/day was tested for 6 months in a large UK primary-care RCT and did not significantly reduce medically attended recurrent UTI
conditions: Blocked recurrent-UTI profile after negative large RCT
